key_insight: A thermal scanning probe can directly write individual perovskite quantum dots with nanometer precision, enabling deterministic single-photon emitters on demand.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to integrate solution-processed quantum emitters with photonic devices, overcoming the randomness that has limited scalable quantum photonic hardware.
method: Create a nanoscale-confined reaction volume using a thermal scanning probe.
Site-selectively synthesize CsPbI3 quantum dots down to a single atomic-scale emitter.
Position emitters with <25 nm spatial control for coupling to photonic cavities.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports high-yield arrays with narrow linewidths and up to 98% single-photon purity at room temperature, plus enhanced spontaneous emission when coupled to cavities.
watch_item: Device performance may still depend on thermal/probe stability and precise control of emitter size, position, and material consistency during writing.
paper_title: Direct writing of individual quantum dots
